mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive equity returns over the next month when the economy operates below full capacity.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% annual threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals robust labor market absorption, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence metric shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth indicates weakening aggregate demand, reducing corporate profit margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration coincides with positive 20-day returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations, invalidating the demand-earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum that translates into short-term positive equity returns through improved business sentiment.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth over any 24-month rolling period.
